msContents.summary:
[p]Liber medicinalis.[/p]
[p]A compendium of the 'Canon' by an unnamed German [?]: including a list of vegetable and animal materia medica, receipts, etc.[/p]
[p]Written in a rounded semi-current gothic hand 33-34 lines to a page. Some initials in red or black, paragraph marks in red.[/p]
[p]Fol. 1. [IN nomine domini cuis bonitate vobis karissimis sociis et scolaribus / meis debitor sum ad erudiendium in moribus et scientiis] / quia prius vobis tradidi compendium moralium in quo / continentur summe philosophorum et theologorum atque vobis tradidi / breuiter summas librorum aristotelis maioris philosophie et paruorum naturalium / nunc quidem secundum promissionem vobis a me factam volo impartire / vtiliora et meliora artis medicine cum experimentis et secretis / que uel quas maiores medici habuerunt christo adiuuan / te me In cuius nomine hoc opus aggrediar. Capitulum primum / de vtilitate medicine et de quibus considerandum est in medicina. / DIco in primis quod medicina est scientia qua sanitas et insanitas / cognoscitur qua sanitas conseruatur ...
[p]35v, line 23 MEdicina simplex est que non est cum alia medicina / per manum artificis commicta seu composita / ... [/p]
[p]77v, line 23 ... vulpis qui desiccatur et bibitus confert asmati et cetera. line 23 Sequitur Tractatus tertius de cibis et potibus.[/p]
[p] 129, line 2 ... sufficiet ad curandum omnem morbum / curabilem. Si autem volueris pluria videre recurre ad / alios libros auicenne et galieni et aliorum medicorum / autenticorum. Sit igitur deus benedictus et gloriosus qui / ad finem diu desideratum produxit. Amen. anno [14]94.[/p]
[p] line 7 Verses in Latin on medicinal weights, receipts, etc.[/p]
[p]129v-End Medical receipts, etc.[/p]
[p]Ff. 1-35v Medicina simplex. 35v-77v Food and drink. 80-89v Fevers. 89v-122v Treatment of diseases, poisons. 122v-129 Syrups, electuaries, etc.[/p]

msItem.author: Avicenna, 980-1036
msItem.title: Avicenna (980-1036)
msItem.filiation: The suggestion that the MS. was written at Amberg, as stated in Sotheby's catlogue, seems doubtful, and the expansion 'Amen' for the original 'Am' seems more probable.

objectDesc.supportDesc: 1 volumeThe binding is very well preserved and there is a similar example from the Erfürt Town Library which is reproduced by Loubier [i]Der Bucheinband[/i], 1926. Fig. 10 on p. 9.

binding.p: The binding is very well preserved and there is a similar example from the Erfürt Town Library which is reproduced by Loubier [i]Der Bucheinband[/i], 1926. Fig. 10 on p. 9.

bibl.title: Catalogue of Western Manuscripts on Medicine and Science in the Wellcome Historical Medical Library
bibl.author: S. A. J. Moorat
bibl.publisher: Wellcome Institute for the History of Medicine

custodialHist.custEvent: Database description transcribed from S.A.J. Moorat, [i]Catalogue of Western Manuscripts on Medicine and Science in the Wellcome Historical Medical Library[/i] (London: Wellcome Institute for the History of Medicine, 1962-1973).

listBibl.bibl: At Wellcome Collection: This MS. which is lettered on the upper cover 'Liber medicinalis' agrees almost verbatim with the contents of MS. No. 105 [Avicenna] though the order of the text is different, and the 'Compendium de regimine sanitatis' is not included.

revisionDesc.change: Transformed Calm xml export to TEI xml file using a transformation scenario
Manual edits following automated Calm-TEI transformation
